label: Toe abrasion without diabetic foot or infection
summary: You were treated for a toe abrasion.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a superficial toe abrasion. We did not find a deep wound, tendon injury, debris concern, or infection today.
home: Keep the area clean and dry today.
Wash gently with soap and water when your clinician says it is okay.
Do not scrub, pick, or dig at the wound.
meds: Use antibiotic ointment, dressings, or pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended.
Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen only if you can take it safely and follow the label.
return: Fever, pus, spreading redness, red streaks, worsening warmth, or swelling.
Bleeding that will not stop with firm pressure.
New numbness, weakness, color change, worsening pain, or concern that something is stuck in the wound.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or the ED if the wound is not improving, if you were told to recheck, or if tetanus questions remain.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of superficial toe abrasion.
No deep wound, repair-needed laceration, tendon/nerve/vessel injury, retained foreign body, infection, bite, burn, diabetic foot, or specialist wound plan.
exclusion: Deep wound, repair-needed laceration, bite wound, dirty wound, crush injury, burn or chemical wound, retained foreign body, tendon/nerve/vessel injury, wound infection, diabetic foot risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed wound plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports cuts and puncture wound aftercare, keeping wounds clean, and escalation for infection, bleeding, retained foreign body, or deeper structure concerns. This expansion is limited to a clinician-diagnosed superficial abrasion without deep injury, infection, bite, burn, diabetic foot, pregnancy/pediatric, or specialist-directed wound pathways.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of superficial toe abrasion
superficial abrasion only
no infection
no deep wound or debris concern

label: Minor inner-cheek bite without deep laceration or infection
summary: Your symptoms fit minor inner cheek bite.
found: Your clinician diagnosed minor inner cheek bite. We did not find infection, deep injury, or another emergency skin or mouth problem today.
home: Keep the area clean and dry.
Avoid the friction, product, or trigger that seems to be irritating the area.
Use gentle skin or mouth care only as your clinician recommended.
meds: Use creams, ointments, rinses, itch medicine, or pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended.
Do not use leftover antibiotics or steroid medicine unless your clinician told you to.
return: Fever, spreading redness, warmth, pus, red streaks, rapidly worsening pain, or swelling.
Mouth sores that spread, trouble swallowing, trouble opening your mouth, or face or neck swelling.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dentistry, dermatology, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor inner cheek bite.
No infection, abscess, deep wound, mucosal emergency, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Cellulitis, abscess, deep wound, severe or spreading rash, systemic illness, mucosal emergency, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
must_not_miss: Deep-space oral infection when mouth symptoms are present.
Retained foreign body or deep wound.

terms: clinician diagnosis minor inner cheek bite
discharge diagnosis minor inner cheek bite

high_confidence_terms: clinician diagnosis minor inner cheek bite
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis and minor wound-care framing, trigger avoidance or gentle care, clinician-directed topical or symptomatic treatment, and escalation for infection, worsening symptoms, severe reactions, or deeper injury concerns. This expansion is limited to a clinician-diagnosed localized irritation without infection, abscess, deep injury, pregnancy/pediatric, or specialist-directed pathways.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of minor inner cheek bite
localized irritation only
no infection or abscess
no deep injury or specialist-directed plan
